introSection: Wildcat was a wooden roller coaster at Seabreeze Amusement Park built in 1926 by the Philadelphia Toboggan Company. The roller coaster operated until 1935 when it was destroyed by a large fire that consumed much of the park's southern end. Wildcat stood in a section of the park where the iconic, "Over The Falls" log flume ride and today's log flume currently stands. The coaster passed directly alongside the Jack Rabbit's station. The Wildcat's station was located North of Jack Rabbit where the Flying Scooters flat ride is today.
fullText: Wildcat was a wooden roller coaster at Seabreeze Amusement Park built in 1926 by the Philadelphia Toboggan Company. The roller coaster operated until 1935 when it was destroyed by a large fire that consumed much of the park's southern end. Wildcat stood in a section of the park where the iconic, "Over The Falls" log flume ride and today's log flume currently stands. The coaster passed directly alongside the Jack Rabbit's station. The Wildcat's station was located North of Jack Rabbit where the Flying Scooters flat ride is today.
